content: To lower cholesterol, losing weight is the best approach for those who are overweight or obese; use a BMI calculator to assess weight status.

Addressing inflammation involves managing body composition through regular exercise, quitting smoking, and reducing intake of refined foods; high sensitivity C-reactive protein (hs-CRP) levels over 1 indicate inflammation.

Checking thyroid status is important; a full thyroid panel including TSH, free and total thyroid hormones, reverse T3, and antithyroid antibodies should be utilized by a knowledgeable healthcare provider.
Increasing dietary fiber through unrefined plant foods is beneficial; aim to consume more unprocessed plant-based foods to enhance fiber intake.
Replacing fat with carbohydrate can help lower cholesterol; test this approach if your cholesterol is very high and you are consuming a high-fat, low-carbohydrate diet.
